note: 8C-1 CBraMod primary / CodeBrain exploratory. See slope summary + trends; interpretation per FSR_48 v2 grid.
l1_trend_primary: persists_high_flat (~0.95 near-ceiling; diversity does NOT reduce subject separability; ceiling-bounded caveat)
l5_reliance_trend_primary: flat (~0; no reliance scaling; on gated ~half of cells)
headline: Over the feasible range, cross-subject transfer improves with more source DATA (growing-trials, perf slope +0.036 CI[0.028,0.045]) but NOT with subject DIVERSITY at fixed data (fixed-trials FLAT over {2,4,8}). Mean pairwise subject separability stays near-ceiling ~0.95 and FLAT across N_source (subject strongly encoded throughout; diversity does not reduce it). Subject-subspace reliance FLAT (~0). => the gain is a SAMPLE-SIZE effect; subject information PERSISTS; reliance does NOT scale. Extends 8B (subject encoded != harmful lever) to a scaling setting. Architecture-consistent (CodeBrain exploratory same direction, weaker/weaker-task).
caveats: task WEAK on PhysioNetMI frozen (target 0.52-0.60, gate pass cbramod 0.52 / codebrain 0.39) -> L4/L5/L6 on gated ~half of cells; similar to BNCI not SHU-MI
fixed-trials only {2,4,8} (3 points); growing includes leveraged N=all
CodeBrain exploratory (gate 0.39): same direction, weaker task
proceed_note: Interesting FSR claims (subject-DIVERSITY effect, reliance scaling) are NULL; only signal is sample-size (mundane) + persistent L1 + flat L5. Per PM: null-for-diversity -> do NOT add specialist baselines.
